hero.one_line_summary: A delegate represents other people, while the verb delegate means giving another person a task or some authority.
hero.memorable_example: Effective managers know when to delegate routine tasks to other team members.

meanings.definition: a person chosen or elected to represent a group at a meeting, conference, or organization
examples: Each country sent several delegates to the international conference.
The union delegates voted in favor of the proposed agreement.
She attended the convention as a delegate from her university.
Delegates from more than fifty nations participated in the climate summit.
The committee elected two student delegates to present its concerns.
Conference delegates received copies of the final report.
The delegate spoke on behalf of workers in the manufacturing sector.
meanings.plain_english_equivalent: a person who represents a group
meanings.typical_pattern: a delegate to + event or a delegate from + group or place
meanings.register: neutral to formal; common in politics, conferences, unions, institutions, and international organizations

nuance.central_nuance: A delegate does not attend only as a private individual; the person represents the interests or views of a larger group.
nuance.usual_implication: The delegate may be expected to speak, negotiate, or vote on behalf of the group.

grammar.how_to_use: Use a delegate for one representative and delegates for more than one.
grammar.what_can_follow: Use to before the meeting or organization attended, and from before the place or group represented.
grammar.preposition_note: Say a delegate to the conference but a delegate from the university.
grammar.noun_use: This is a countable noun, so the singular normally needs a, an, or the.

patterns.pattern: a delegate to + conference or meeting
patterns.use: Use this to identify the event or organization that the person attends as a representative.
patterns.example: He was selected as a delegate to the national convention.

patterns.pattern: a delegate from + country, institution, or group
patterns.use: Use this to identify the people or place that the delegate represents.
patterns.example: A delegate from the teachers' union addressed the committee.

patterns.use: Use this when a group chooses someone to represent it.
patterns.example: Each department elected a delegate to the university council.

patterns.use: Use this for the people officially attending a conference as representatives.
patterns.example: Conference delegates discussed several proposals on the final day.

errors.wrong: She attended the meeting as delegate.
errors.correct: She attended the meeting as a delegate.
errors.explanation: The singular countable noun normally needs a.

errors.wrong: He was a delegate of the international conference.
errors.correct: He was a delegate to the international conference.
errors.explanation: Use delegate to for the event attended.

errors.wrong: She was a delegate to her university.
errors.correct: She was a delegate from her university.
errors.explanation: Use delegate from for the group or institution represented.

meanings.definition: to give a task or responsibility to another person so that they do it on your behalf
examples: Effective managers know when to delegate routine tasks to other team members.
The professor delegated responsibility for collecting the data to two research assistants.
She delegated the preparation of the monthly report to her deputy.
The team leader delegated each stage of the project to a different specialist.
You should delegate some of your work instead of trying to do everything yourself.
The director was reluctant to delegate important responsibilities.
By delegating administrative tasks, doctors can spend more time with patients.
meanings.plain_english_equivalent: give part of your work to someone else
meanings.typical_pattern: delegate + task or responsibility + to + person
meanings.register: neutral to formal; common in business, management, education, and professional contexts

nuance.central_nuance: Delegating means assigning work to another person while usually remaining responsible for the overall result.
nuance.usual_implication: Good delegation involves choosing a suitable person and giving them enough information or authority to complete the task.
nuance.contrast_explanation: Assign is a general word for giving someone a task. Delegate often suggests that the task originally belonged to a person with greater responsibility or authority.

grammar.how_to_use: This verb normally needs the task or responsibility after it.
grammar.what_can_follow: It is often followed by task, duty, responsibility, work, decision, or the name of an activity.
grammar.preposition_note: Use to before the person receiving the task, as in delegate the work to an assistant.
grammar.position_note: The task normally comes before to and the person: delegate a task to someone.

patterns.pattern: delegate + task or duty + to + person
patterns.use: Use this when giving a particular piece of work to someone else.
patterns.example: The manager delegated the task to an experienced employee.

patterns.pattern: delegate responsibility for + noun or -ing form + to + person
patterns.use: Use this when another person becomes responsible for a particular activity.
patterns.example: She delegated responsibility for organizing the event to her assistant.

patterns.pattern: delegate the preparation or management of + noun
patterns.use: Use this for formal descriptions of work given to another person.
patterns.example: The director delegated the management of the project to a senior engineer.

patterns.pattern: learn or be willing to delegate
patterns.use: Use this without an object when the type of work is already clear.
patterns.example: Successful leaders must learn to delegate.

errors.wrong: The manager delegated to Sara the report.
errors.correct: The manager delegated the report to Sara.
errors.explanation: The usual order is delegate the task to the person.

errors.wrong: She delegated her assistant to prepare the report.
errors.correct: She delegated the preparation of the report to her assistant.
errors.explanation: It is usually more natural to delegate a task or responsibility to someone.

errors.wrong: He delegated about organizing the conference.
errors.correct: He delegated responsibility for organizing the conference.
errors.explanation: Do not use about after delegate. Name the task or responsibility directly.

meanings.definition: to officially give part of your authority or decision-making power to another person or organization
examples: The director delegated authority to approve small purchases to department managers.
National governments sometimes delegate certain powers to regional authorities.
The law allows the agency to delegate some of its responsibilities to local councils.
Senior officials may delegate decision-making authority to local administrators.
The board delegated control of daily operations to the executive director.
Parliament can delegate limited regulatory powers to government agencies.
The president delegated responsibility for the negotiations to the foreign minister.
meanings.plain_english_equivalent: officially give someone part of your power
meanings.typical_pattern: delegate + authority, power, control, or responsibility + to + person or organization
meanings.register: formal; common in law, government, administration, and organizational management

nuance.central_nuance: This meaning concerns official power rather than merely the performance of a task.
nuance.usual_implication: The original authority often keeps overall responsibility and may place limits on the power being transferred.
nuance.contrast_explanation: To surrender authority is to give it up, often completely. To delegate authority is usually to transfer only part of it while keeping overall control or responsibility.

grammar.how_to_use: This verb normally needs the authority, power, control, or responsibility being transferred after it.
grammar.what_can_follow: Common objects include authority, power, control, responsibility, decision-making, and functions.
grammar.preposition_note: Use to before the person, department, or organization receiving the authority.
grammar.position_note: The usual order is delegate authority to someone.

patterns.pattern: delegate authority to + person or organization
patterns.use: Use this when someone officially gives another party the power to make certain decisions.
patterns.example: The minister delegated authority to regional officials.

patterns.pattern: delegate power over + area + to + body
patterns.use: Use this to identify both the area of authority and the organization receiving it.
patterns.example: The law delegates power over local transportation to municipal governments.

patterns.use: Use this when another person or group is allowed to make particular choices.
patterns.example: The company delegated decision-making responsibility to local branches.

patterns.pattern: be delegated to + person or organization
patterns.use: Use the passive form when the transferred authority is the main focus.
patterns.example: Certain administrative powers were delegated to the committee.

errors.wrong: The board delegated authority on the manager.
errors.correct: The board delegated authority to the manager.
errors.explanation: Use to for the person or organization receiving authority.

errors.wrong: The government delegated the local council with new powers.
errors.correct: The government delegated new powers to the local council.
errors.explanation: The usual pattern is delegate something to someone.

errors.wrong: The director delegated all responsibility and was no longer accountable.
errors.correct: The director delegated several responsibilities but remained accountable for the final results.
errors.explanation: Delegating work or authority does not necessarily remove the original person's overall responsibility.

word_family.definition: a group of people chosen to represent others, or the act of giving tasks or authority to another person
examples: A delegation of scientists met with government officials.
Effective delegation allows managers to focus on their most important responsibilities.
The international delegation visited several research institutions.
Poor delegation can leave employees confused about their responsibilities.
word_family.relationship_to_headword: This noun can refer either to a group of delegates or to the process of delegating work or authority.
word_family.grammar_pattern: a delegation from + place, a delegation of + representatives, or the delegation of + task or authority
word_family.usage_note: Use a delegation for a representative group. Delegation is often uncountable when it means the management process.

collocations.example: Each conference delegate received a copy of the research summary.
collocations.meaning: a person officially attending a conference as a representative

collocations.example: She was selected as a delegate to an international education conference.
collocations.meaning: a representative chosen to attend a conference
collocations.grammar_pattern: a delegate to + meeting, conference, or convention

collocations.example: A delegate from Brazil presented the proposal.
collocations.meaning: a representative of a particular country or group
collocations.grammar_pattern: a delegate from + country, institution, or organization

collocations.example: The supervisor delegated the task to a junior researcher.
collocations.meaning: give a piece of work to another person

collocations.example: Managers should delegate responsibility without losing oversight.
collocations.meaning: make another person responsible for completing particular work
collocations.grammar_pattern: delegate responsibility for + noun or -ing form + to + person

collocations.example: Leaders who delegate effectively can use their time more efficiently.
collocations.meaning: give tasks to suitable people in a clear and useful way

collocations.example: The chief executive delegated authority to several regional managers.
collocations.grammar_pattern: delegate authority to + person or organization

collocations.example: The constitution permits the national government to delegate limited powers to local authorities.
collocations.meaning: officially give another person or organization certain powers
collocations.grammar_pattern: delegate power to + person or organization

collocations.example: The officer acted within the limits of her delegated authority.
collocations.meaning: authority officially transferred from a higher authority
collocations.usage_note: Delegated is an adjective in this expression.

collocations.example: A delegation of officials traveled to the neighboring country.
Effective delegation improves both productivity and staff development.
collocations.meaning: the successful assignment of work and responsibility to other people

synonyms.difference: Representative is a broad term for anyone who acts or speaks for others. Delegate often refers specifically to someone formally chosen to attend a meeting, conference, or organization.
synonyms.example: Each organization sent a representative to the meeting.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both words describe someone who acts on behalf of a larger group.

synonyms.difference: Assign simply means give someone a task. Delegate often suggests that a person passes on work that originally belonged to them while keeping overall responsibility.
synonyms.example: The professor assigned each student a separate research topic.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both verbs can mean giving work to another person.

synonyms.difference: Entrust emphasizes confidence that someone will take care of an important task, object, or person. Delegate focuses on transferring responsibility for work.
synonyms.example: The director entrusted her assistant with the confidential documents.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both verbs can describe giving another person responsibility.

synonyms.difference: Authorize means officially give permission to do something. Delegate means transfer some of your own authority or responsibility to another party.
synonyms.example: The committee authorized the director to sign the agreement.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both verbs can involve officially giving someone power to act.

confusables.key_difference: Delegate means give someone a task or authority. Designate usually means officially choose, name, or identify someone or something for a role or purpose.
confusables.headword_example: The director delegated responsibility for the project to Maria.
confusables.confusable_example: The director designated Maria as the project leader.
confusables.why_confused: Both formal verbs can involve giving someone a role, but they describe different actions.

etymology.origin: Delegate comes from Latin delegare, meaning to send as a representative or assign responsibility.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: A _____ of university officials attended the international meeting.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: A delegation of university officials attended the international meeting.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use delegation for a representative group.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: Effective _____ can prevent managers from becoming overwhelmed.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: Effective delegation can prevent managers from becoming overwhelmed.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use delegation for the process of giving work to other people.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: Each department selected a _____ to attend the council meeting.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: Each department selected a delegate to attend the council meeting.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use delegate for the chosen representative.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The manager delegated the task to an experienced employee.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Delegate a task to someone is the standard pattern.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: She attended the conference as delegate from her university.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: She attended the conference as a delegate from her university.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: The singular countable noun requires a.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The board delegated authority on the regional director.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: The board delegated authority to the regional director.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Use to before the person receiving the authority.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: Good leaders are willing to delegate.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Delegate can appear without an object when the type of work is clear from the context.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The supervisor decided to delegate the task _____ a more experienced employee.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The supervisor decided to delegate the task to a more experienced employee.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The two departments each elected a _____ to the university council.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The two departments each elected a delegate to the university council.
collocation_practice.explanation: A delegate to a council is a person chosen to attend it as a representative.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The law allows the ministry to delegate _____ to local agencies.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The law allows the ministry to delegate authority to local agencies.
collocation_practice.explanation: Delegate authority means officially transfer some decision-making power.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: Effective _____ requires clear instructions and appropriate supervision.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: Effective delegation requires clear instructions and appropriate supervision.
collocation_practice.explanation: Effective delegation is the successful assignment of work to others.
